29 CODIFY NORTH CAROLINA PATRIOT STAR FAMILY SCHOLARSHIP PROGRAM
30 SECTION 8A.9.(a) Article 23 of Chapter 116 of the General Statutes is amended by
31 adding the following new Part to read:
32 "Part 9. North Carolina Patriot Star Family Scholarship Program.
33 "§ 116-209.120. Program established.
34 The Board of Governors of The University of North Carolina shall establish the North
35 Carolina Patriot Star Family Scholarship Program. To the extent funds are made available for the
36 Program, the Board shall award funds for the purpose of administering scholarships under the
37 Program to (i) the Patriot Foundation, a nonprofit corporation, and (ii) the Marine Corps
38 Scholarship Foundation, Inc., a nonprofit corporation.
39 "§ 116-209.122. Purpose of the Program.
40 The Patriot Foundation and the Marine Corps Scholarship Foundation, Inc., respectively,
41 shall provide for scholarships to eligible children and eligible spouses of certain veterans, eligible
42 children of certain currently serving members of the Armed Forces, and eligible disabled veterans
43 to attend eligible postsecondary institutions in accordance with the requirements of this Part.
44 "§ 116-209.124. Definitions.
45 For the purposes of this Part, the following definitions shall apply:
46 (1) Armed Forces. – A component of the United States Army, Navy, Marine
47 Corps, Air Force, Space Force, and Coast Guard, including their reserve
48 components.
49 (2) Eligible child or eligible children. – Any person who meets all of the following
50 requirements:
Page 154 Senate Bill 257-Second Edition
General Assembly Of North Carolina Session 2025
1 a. Is attending or has been accepted to enroll in an eligible postsecondary
2 institution.
3 b. Is a legal resident of North Carolina when scholarship documentation
4 is completed, provided that if a child is claimed as a dependent by the
5 child's parent, residency may be established based on a parent meeting
6 the requirements of sub-sub-sub-subdivision IV. of
7 sub-sub-subdivision 1. of sub-subdivision d. of this subdivision.
8 c. Has complied with the requirements of the Selective Service System,
9 if applicable.
10 d. The parent of the person is a veteran or a currently serving member of
11 the Armed Forces that meets all of the following criteria:
12 1. One of the following residency conditions:
13 I. Is a resident of North Carolina at the time of
14 scholarship documentation completion.
15 II. Was a resident of North Carolina at the time of entrance
16 into service in the Armed Forces.
17 III. Was permanently stationed in North Carolina at the
18 time of his or her death.
19 IV. Is an active duty service member permanently stationed
20 in North Carolina at the time of documentation
21 completion.
22 2. One of the following service conditions:
23 I. Was a member of the Armed Forces who was killed in
24 action or in the line of duty or died of wounds or other
25 causes not due to the service member's willful
26 misconduct during a period of war, national
27 emergency, or training in preparation for future
28 conflicts and is a direct result of service in the line of
29 duty.
30 II. Was a member of the Armed Forces who died of
31 service-connected injuries, wounds, illness, or other
32 causes incurred or aggravated while a member of the
33 Armed Forces during a period of war, national
34 emergency, or training in preparation for future
35 conflicts and is a direct result of service in the line of
36 duty. Standard documentation of the parent's death,
37 wounds, injury, or illness shall be supplied by a
38 scholarship recipient at the time of scholarship request.
39 III. Is a veteran of the Armed Forces who meets both of the
40 following criteria:
41 A. Incurred traumatic injuries or wounds or
42 sustained a major illness while a member of the
43 Armed Forces during a period of war, national
44 emergency, or training in preparation for future
45 conflicts, and the injuries, wounds, or illness are
46 a direct result of service in the line of duty.
47 B. Is receiving compensation of at least fifty
48 percent (50%) as rated by the U.S. Department
49 of Veterans Affairs for a disability connected to
50 the injuries, wounds, or illness identified in
Senate Bill 257-Second Edition Page 155
General Assembly Of North Carolina Session 2025
1 accordance with sub-sub-sub-subdivision I. of
2 this sub-sub-subdivision.
3 IV. Is a current member of the Armed Forces who incurred
4 traumatic injuries or wounds or sustained a major
5 illness while a member of the Armed Forces during a
6 period of war, national emergency, or training in
7 preparation for future conflicts, and the injuries,
8 wounds, or illness are a direct result of service in the
9 line of duty. The parent's traumatic wounds, injury, or
10 major illness must be documented by the member's
11 Unit Commander.
12 (3) Eligible disabled veteran. – Any person who meets all of the following
13 criteria:
14 a. Is a parent of an eligible child pursuant to sub-sub-sub-subdivision III.
15 of sub-sub-subdivision 2. of sub-subdivision d. of subdivision (2) of
16 this subsection.
17 b. Is a resident of North Carolina when scholarship documentation is
18 completed.
19 c. Is attending or has been accepted to enroll in an eligible postsecondary
20 institution.
21 (4) Eligible postsecondary institution. – Any of the following postsecondary
22 educational institutions:
23 a. A constituent institution of The University of North Carolina.
24 b. A community college under the jurisdiction of the State Board of
25 Community Colleges.
26 c. A private educational institution as defined in G.S. 143B-1224.
27 d. A private vocational institution, including Federal Aviation
28 Administration certificated aviation training programs.
29 (5) Eligible spouse. – Any person who meets all of the following criteria:
30 a. Is attending or has been accepted to enroll in an eligible postsecondary
31 institution.
32 b. Is a legal resident of North Carolina when scholarship documentation
33 is completed.
34 c. Has complied with the requirements of the Selective Service System,
35 if applicable.
36 d. Meets one of the conditions set forth in sub-sub-sub-subdivisions I.
37 through III. of sub-sub-subdivision 2. of sub-subdivision d. of
38 subdivision (2) of this subsection.
39 (6) Program. – The North Carolina Patriot Star Family Scholarship Program
40 established pursuant to G.S. 116-209.122.
41 (7) Veteran. – An individual who has served and is no longer serving in the Armed
42 Forces of the United States. For the purposes of this subdivision, the veteran
43 shall have separated from the Armed Forces under honorable conditions or
44 whose death or disability of at least fifty percent (50%) or more was incurred
45 as a direct result of service in the line of duty.
46 "§ 116-209.126. Administration; awards.
47 (a) To the extent funds are made available for the Program, the Patriot Foundation and
48 the Marine Corps Scholarship Foundation, Inc., shall each separately administer and award
49 scholarships to eligible applicants in accordance with the requirements of the Program. In
50 administering the Program, each nonprofit corporation shall be responsible for oversight for the
Page 156 Senate Bill 257-Second Edition
General Assembly Of North Carolina Session 2025
1 scholarships awarded through its organization under the Program to ensure compliance with the
2 provisions of this Part.
3 (b) Each nonprofit corporation shall, at a minimum, establish criteria and procedures
4 related to scholarship documentation completion, the amount of individual scholarships, the
5 permissible uses of scholarship funds, the period of eligibility for award of a scholarship, the
6 conditions for a revocation of a scholarship, and any other procedures it deems necessary for its
7 administration of the Program.
8 (c) If an eligible child or eligible spouse receives a scholarship or other grant covering
9 the costs of attendance at an eligible postsecondary institution for which the scholarship is
10 awarded, then the amount of a scholarship awarded under this Part shall be reduced so that the
11 sum of all grants and scholarships covering the costs of attendance received by the eligible child
12 or eligible spouse does not exceed the costs of attendance for the institution. For the purposes of
13 this section, costs of attendance shall include monies for tuition, fees, books, supplies, and
14 school-related expenses, including laptops, equipment, tutoring support, as well as room and
15 board, as long as the scholarship recipient is enrolled as at least a half-time student at the
16 institution. Off-campus housing costs for room and board are also included to the extent the
17 eligible postsecondary institution includes it in its costs of attendance.
18 "§ 116-209.128. Reporting.
19 (a) The Patriot Foundation shall submit a report by April 1 of each year in which the
20 Patriot Foundation spends State funds made available for the Program to the Joint Legislative
21 Education Oversight Committee and the Fiscal Research Division on the activities related to the
22 Program and the use of the State funds.
23 (b) The Marine Corps Scholarship Foundation, Inc., shall submit a report by April 1 of
24 each year in which the Marine Corps Scholarship Foundation, Inc., spends State funds made
25 available for the Program to the Joint Legislative Education Oversight Committee and the Fiscal
26 Research Division on the activities related to the Program and the use of the State funds."

13 REPORTS BY NON-STATE ENTITIES ON THE USE OF DIRECTED GRANT FUNDS
14 SECTION 9B.1. The Department of Health and Human Services shall submit to the
15 Joint Legislative Oversight Committee on Health and Human Services and the Fiscal Research
16 Division all reports received under 9 NCAC 03M .0205 from non-State entities, as defined in
17 G.S. 143C-1-1, that are recipients of nonrecurring funds allocated in this Part as a directed grant
18 according to the following schedule:
19 (1) By November 1, 2026, all reports on the use of directed grant funds received
20 under this Part for the 2025-2026 fiscal year.
21 (2) By November 1, 2027, all reports on the use of directed grant funds received
22 under this Part for the 2026-2027 fiscal year.
23
24 COMMUNITY HEALTH GRANT PROGRAM
25 SECTION 9B.2.(a) Funds appropriated in this act to the Department of Health and
26 Human Services, Division of Central Management, Office of Rural Health, for each year of the
27 2025-2027 fiscal biennium for the Community Health Grant Program shall be used to continue
28 to administer the Community Health Grant Program as modified by Section 11A.8 of S.L.
29 2017-57.
30 SECTION 9B.2.(b) The Office of Rural Health shall make the final decision about
31 awarding grants under this Program, but no single grant award shall exceed one hundred fifty
32 thousand dollars ($150,000) during the fiscal year. In awarding grants, the Office of Rural Health
33 shall consider the availability of other funds for the applicant; the incidence of poverty in the area
34 served by the applicant or the number of indigent clients served by the applicant; the availability
35 of, or arrangements for, after-hours care; and collaboration between the applicant and a
36 community hospital or other safety net organizations.
37 SECTION 9B.2.(c) Grant recipients shall not use these funds to do any of the
38 following:
39 (1) Enhance or increase compensation or other benefits of personnel,
40 administrators, directors, consultants, or any other persons receiving funds for
41 program administration; provided, however, funds may be used to hire or
42 retain health care providers. The use of grant funds for this purpose does not
43 obligate the Department of Health and Human Services to continue to fund
44 compensation beyond the grant period.
45 (2) Supplant existing funds, including federal funds traditionally received by
46 federally qualified community health centers. However, grant funds may be
47 used to supplement existing programs that serve the purposes described in
48 subsection (a) of this section.
49 (3) Finance or satisfy any existing debt.
Senate Bill 257-Second Edition Page 159
General Assembly Of North Carolina Session 2025
1 SECTION 9B.2.(d) The Office of Rural Health may use up to two hundred thousand
2 dollars ($200,000) of these recurring funds for each year of the 2025-2027 fiscal biennium for
3 administrative purposes.
4 SECTION 9B.2.(e) By September 1 of each year, the Office of Rural Health shall
5 submit a report to the Joint Legislative Oversight Committee on Health and Human Services on
6 community health grants that includes at least all of the following information:
7 (1) The identity and a brief description of each grantee and each program or
8 service offered by the grantee.
9 (2) The amount of funding awarded to each grantee.
10 (3) The number of individuals served by each grantee and, for the individuals
11 served, the types of services provided to each.
12 (4) Any other information requested by the Office of Rural Health as necessary
13 for evaluating the success of the Community Health Grant Program.

15 HEALTH INFORMATION EXCHANGE ACT REVISIONS
16 SECTION 9B.3.(a) Article 29B of Chapter 90 of the General Statutes reads as
17 rewritten:
18 "Article 29B.
19 "Statewide Health Information Exchange Act.
20 "§ 90-414.1. Title.
21 This act Article shall be known and may be cited as the "Statewide Health Information
22 Exchange Act."
23 "§ 90-414.2. Purpose.
24 This Article is intended to improve the quality of health care delivery within this State by
25 facilitating and regulating the use of a voluntary, statewide health information exchange network
26 for the secure electronic transmission of individually identifiable health information among
27 health care providers, health plans, and health care clearinghouses clearinghouses, and the State
28 in a manner that is consistent with the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act,
29 Privacy Rule and Security Rule, 45 C.F.R. §§ 160, 164.
30 "§ 90-414.3. Definitions.
31 The following definitions apply in this Article:
32 (1) Annual compliance report. – The annual report required by G.S. 90-414.13.
33 (1a) Business associate. – As defined in 45 C.F.R. § 160.103.
34 (2) Business associate contract. – The documentation required by 45 C.F.R. §
35 164.502(e)(2) that meets the applicable requirements of 45 C.F.R. §
36 164.504(e).
37 (3) Covered entity. – Any entity described in 45 C.F.R. § 160.103 or any other
38 facility or practitioner licensed by the State to provide health care services.
39 (3a) Data transfer systems. – Electronic systems or platforms that (i) facilitate the
40 submission of any combination of clinical, demographic, or claims data to the
41 HIE Network and (ii) are maintained, controlled, directed, or licensed by, or
42 on behalf of, a covered entity or hybrid entity subject to this Article. Data
43 transfer systems may be comprised of health information technology or claims
44 processing technology, or both, including hardware, software, integrated
45 technologies and related licenses, or packaged solutions sold as services. Data
46 transfer systems include, but are not limited to, electronic systems or platforms
47 related to electronic health records, pharmacy benefits and claims, claims
48 processing, or care management. Data transfer systems do not include any
49 information technology systems that are directly maintained, controlled, or
50 licensed by the State Health Plan for Teachers and State Employees.
51 (4) Department. – North Carolina Department of Health and Human Services.
Page 160 Senate Bill 257-Second Edition
General Assembly Of North Carolina Session 2025
1 (5) Disclose or disclosure. – The release, transfer, provision of access to, or
2 divulging in any other manner an individual's protected health information
3 through the HIE Network.
4 (6) Repealed by Session Laws 2017-57, s. 11A.5(f), effective July 1, 2017.
5 (7) GDAC. – The North Carolina Government Data Analytics Center.
6 (8) HIE Network. – The voluntary, statewide health information exchange
7 network network, which is a health data utility overseen and administered by
8 the Authority.
9 (9) HIPAA. – Sections 261 through 264 of the federal Health Insurance
10 Portability and Accountability Act of 1996, P.L. 104-191, as amended, and
11 any federal regulations adopted to implement these sections, as amended.
12 (10) Individual. – As defined in 45 C.F.R. § 160.103.
13 (11) North Carolina Health Information Exchange Advisory Board or Advisory
14 Board. – The Advisory Board established under G.S. 90-414.8.
15 (12) North Carolina Health Information Exchange Authority or Authority. – The
16 entity established pursuant to G.S. 90-414.7.
17 (13) Opt out. – An individual's affirmative decision communicated to the Authority
18 in writing to disallow his or her protected health information from being
19 disclosed by the Authority to covered entities or other persons or entities
20 through the HIE Network.
21 (13a) Organization National Provider Identifier or Organization NPI. – The HIPAA
22 Administrative Simplification Standard that utilizes a 10-position all-numeric
23 identification number assigned by the federal National Provider System to
24 uniquely identify a health care provider that is an entity other than an
25 individual human being that furnishes health care.
26 (14) Protected health information. – As defined in 45 C.F.R. § 160.103.
27 (15) Public health purposes. – The public health activities and purposes described
28 in 45 C.F.R. § 164.512(b).
29 (16) Qualified organization. – An entity with which the Authority has contracted
30 for the sole purpose of facilitating the exchange of data with or through the
31 HIE Network.
32 (17) Research purposes. – Research purposes referenced in and subject to the
33 standards described in 45 C.F.R. § 164.512(i).
34 (18) State CIO. – The State Chief Information Officer.
35 (19) State-funded health care. – Means all of the following:
36 a. The North Carolina Medicaid program.
37 b. The State Health Plan for Teachers and State Employees.
38 c. Health care facilities and health care programs administered or
39 operated by the Department of Health and Human Services, the
40 Department of Public Safety, or the Department of Adult Correction,
41 and their employees, agents, or grantees.
42 (20) State health care funds. – Monies paid to providers or entities for the provision
43 of health care services to recipients of State-funded health care. The term
44 includes both (i) direct payments from the State to providers and entities and
45 (ii) payments that providers and entities receive from third parties, or the
46 agents of third parties, that are retained by the State for the administration or
47 delivery, or both, of State-funded health care, including prepaid health plans
48 as defined in G.S. 108D-1 and claims processors as defined in G.S. 135-48.1.
49 "§ 90-414.4. Required participation in HIE Network for some providers.
50 (a) Findings. – The General Assembly makes the following findings:
Senate Bill 257-Second Edition Page 161
General Assembly Of North Carolina Session 2025
1 (1) That controlling escalating health care costs of the Medicaid program and
2 other State-funded health care services is of significant importance to the
3 State, its taxpayers, its Medicaid recipients, and other recipients beneficiaries
4 of State-funded health care services.care.
5 (2) That the State and covered entities in North Carolina need timely access to
6 certain demographic and clinical information pertaining to services rendered
7 to Medicaid and other beneficiaries of State-funded health care program
8 beneficiaries and paid for with Medicaid or other State-funded State health
9 care funds in order to assess performance, improve health care outcomes,
10 pinpoint medical expense trends, identify beneficiary health risks, and
11 evaluate how the State is spending money on Medicaid and other State-funded
12 health care services. The care. To that end, the Department of Information
13 Technology, the Department of State Treasurer, State Health Plan Division,
14 and the Department of Health and Human Services, Division of Health
15 Benefits, have an affirmative duty to facilitate and support participation by
16 covered entities in the statewide health information exchange network.
17 (3) That making demographic and clinical information available to the State and
18 covered entities in North Carolina by secure electronic means as set forth in
19 subsection (b) of this section will improve care coordination within and across
20 health systems, increase care quality for such beneficiaries, beneficiaries of
21 State-funded health care, enable more effective population health
22 management, reduce duplication of medical services, augment syndromic
23 surveillance, allow more accurate measurement of care services and
24 outcomes, increase strategic knowledge about the health of the population,
25 and facilitate health care cost containment.
26 (a1) Mandatory Connection to HIE Network. – Notwithstanding the voluntary nature of
27 the HIE Network under G.S. 90-414.2, the following providers and entities shall be connected to
28 the HIE Network and begin submitting data through the HIE Network pertaining to services
29 rendered to Medicaid beneficiaries and to other of State-funded health care program beneficiaries
30 and paid for with Medicaid or other State-funded State health care funds in accordance with the
31 following time line:
32 (1) The following providers of Medicaid services licensed to operate in the State
33 that have an electronic health record system shall begin submitting, at a
34 minimum, demographic and clinical data by June 1, 2018:
35 a. Hospitals as defined in G.S. 131E-176(13).
36 b. Physicians licensed to practice under Article 1 of Chapter 90 of the
37 General Statutes, this Chapter, except for licensed physicians whose
38 primary area of practice is psychiatry.
39 c. Physician assistants as defined in 21 NCAC 32S.0201.
40 d. Nurse practitioners as defined in 21 NCAC 36.0801.
41 (2) Except as provided in subdivisions (3), (4), and (5) of this subsection, all other
42 providers of Medicaid and State-funded health care services and their
43 affiliated entities shall begin submitting demographic and clinical data by
44 January 1, 2023.
45 (3) The following entities shall submit encounter and claims data, as appropriate,
46 in accordance with the following time line:
47 a. Prepaid Health Plans, as defined in G.S. 108D-1, by the
48 commencement date of a capitated contract with the Division of
49 Health Benefits for the delivery of Medicaid services as specified in
50 Article 4 of Chapter 108D of the General Statutes.

1 b. Local management entities/managed care organizations, as defined in
2 G.S. 122C-3, by June 1, 2020.

24 (a2) Extensions of Time for Establishing Connection to the HIE Network. – The
25 Department of Information Technology, in consultation with the Department of Health and
26 Human Services and the State Health Plan for Teachers and State Employees, may establish a
27 process to grant limited extensions of the time for providers and entities to connect to the HIE
28 Network and begin submitting data as required by this section upon the request of a provider or
29 entity that demonstrates an ongoing good-faith effort to take necessary steps to establish such
30 connection and begin data submission as required by this section. The process for granting an
31 extension of time must include a presentation by the provider or entity to the Department of
32 Information Technology, the Department of Health and Human Services, and the State Health
33 Plan for Teachers and State Employees on the expected time line for connecting to the HIE
34 Network and commencing data submission as required by this section. Neither the Department
35 of Information Technology, the Department of Health and Human Services, nor the State Health
36 Plan for Teachers and State Employees shall grant an extension of time (i) to any provider or
37 entity that fails to provide this information to both Departments, and the State Health Plan for
38 Teachers and State Employees, (ii) that would result in the provider or entity connecting to the
39 HIE Network and commencing data submission as required by this section later than January 1,
40 2023. The Department of Information Technology shall consult with the Department of Health
41 and Human Services and the State Health Plan for Teachers and State Employees to review and
42 decide upon a request for an extension of time under this section within 30 days after receiving
43 a request for an extension.
44 (a3) Exemptions from Connecting to the HIE Network. – The Secretary of Health and
45 Human Services, or the Secretary's designee, shall have the authority to grant exemptions to
46 classes of providers of Medicaid and other State-funded health care services for whom acquiring
47 and implementing an electronic health record system and connecting to the HIE Network as
48 required by this section would constitute an undue hardship. The Secretary, or the Secretary's
49 designee, shall promptly notify the Department of Information Technology of classes of
50 providers granted hardship exemptions under this subsection. Neither the Secretary nor the
Senate Bill 257-Second Edition Page 163
General Assembly Of North Carolina Session 2025
1 Secretary's designee shall grant any hardship exemption that would result in any class of provider
2 connecting to the HIE Network and submitting data later than December 31, 2022.
3 (a4) Connected Status. – A provider or entity identified in subsection (a1) of this section
4 is deemed connected to the HIE Network when the covered entity that provides, maintains,
5 controls, directs, or licenses that provider's or entity's data transfer system has done all of the
6 following:
7 (1) Established an operable technical connection with the HIE Network approved
8 by the Authority that supports the submission of required patient data
9 generated by the provider or entity.
10 (2) Provided its Organization NPI to the Authority.
11 (3) Executed with the Authority a valid, written participation agreement pursuant
12 to subdivision (b)(6) of G.S. 90-414.7.
13 (4) Communicated to the Authority, in writing, the identity of all providers and
14 entities on whose behalf it maintains a data transfer system.
15 (5) Either has met or is making reasonable efforts to meet data quality standards
16 established by the Authority that are published on its website.
17 (b) Mandatory Submission of Demographic and Clinical Data. – Notwithstanding the
18 voluntary nature of the HIE Network under G.S. 90-414.2 and, except as otherwise provided in
19 subsection subsections (c) and (c1) of this section, as a condition of receiving State funds,
20 including Medicaid funds, the following entities shall submit at least twice daily, through the
21 HIE network, demographic and clinical information pertaining to services rendered to Medicaid
22 and other beneficiaries of State-funded health care program beneficiaries and paid for with
23 Medicaid or other State-funded State health care funds, solely for the purposes set forth in
24 subsection (a) of this section:
25 (1) Each hospital, as defined in G.S. 131E-176(13) that has an electronic health
26 record system.
27 (2) Each Medicaid provider, unless the provider is an ambulatory surgical center
28 as defined in G.S. 131E-146; however, a physician who performs a procedure
29 at the ambulatory surgical center must be connected to the HIE Network.
30 (3) Each provider that receives State health care funds for the provision of health
31 services, State-funded health care, unless the provider is an ambulatory
32 surgical center as defined in G.S. 131E-146; however, a physician who
33 performs a procedure at the ambulatory surgical center must be connected to
34 the HIE Network.
35 (4) Each prepaid health plan, as defined in G.S. 58-93-5, that is under a capitated
36 contract with the Department for the delivery of Medicaid services, or a local
37 management entity/managed care organization, as defined in
38 G.S. 122C-3.G.S. 122C-3, that is under a capitated prepaid health plan
39 contract with the Department.
40 (b1) Balance Billing Prohibition. – An in-network provider or entity who that (i) renders
41 health care services, including prescription drugs and durable medical equipment, under a
42 contract with the State Health Plan for Teachers and State Employees and who (ii) is not
43 connected to the HIE Network in accordance with this Article, is prohibited from billing the State
44 Health Plan or a Plan member more than either party would be billed if the entity or provider was
45 connected to the HIE Network. Balance billing because the provider or entity did not connect to
46 the HIE Network is prohibited.

48 the disclosure restrictions of 42 C.F.R. § 2 are exempt from the requirements of subsection (b)
49 of this section but only with respect to the patient records subject to these disclosure restrictions.
50 Providers shall comply with the requirements of subsection (b) of this section with respect to all
51 other patient records. A pharmacy shall only be Pharmacies registered with the North Carolina

40 providers.providers, and student health centers that primarily serve students
41 matriculating at public or private institutions of higher education in this State.
42 (11) Chiropractors licensed under Article 8 of this Chapter.
43 (12) Dentists licensed under Article 2 of this Chapter.
44 Connection to the HIE Network by any other covered entities that are not required by
45 subsections (a1) and (b) of this section to connect to the HIE Network or submit data is voluntary.
46 (e1) Mandatory and Voluntary Connection and Submissions by the Same Covered Entity.

5 (f) Confidentiality of Data. – All data submitted to or through the HIE Network
6 containing protected health information, personally identifying information, or a combination of
7 these, that are in the possession of the Department of Information Technology or any other
8 agency of the State are confidential and shall not be defined as public records under G.S. 132-1.
9 This subsection shall not be construed to prohibit the disclosure of any such data as otherwise
10 permitted under federal law.
11 (g) Time-Limited Exceptions for Connecting to, and Submitting Data Through, the HIE
12 Network. – All of the following apply to any exception granted by the Authority for connecting
13 to, and submitting data through, the HIE Network:
14 (1) A covered entity that provides, maintains, controls, directs, or licenses a data
15 transfer system on behalf of providers or entities identified in subsection (a1)
16 of this section may seek to obtain from the Authority a time-limited exception
17 for those providers or entities to connect to, and begin submitting required
18 data through, the HIE Network.
19 (2) The Authority shall administer the process by which a covered entity seeks a
20 time-limited exception for providers or entities to connect to, and begin
21 submitting required data through, the HIE Network. The Authority shall make
22 the final determination about whether to grant or deny requests for a
23 time-limited exception. Any exception authorized by the Authority may not
24 exceed a one-year period. However, a covered entity may seek to renew an
25 exception.
26 (3) In order for a covered entity to obtain a time-limited exception for the
27 providers and entities on whose behalf it provides, maintains, controls, directs,
28 or licenses a data transfer system, the covered entity must demonstrate
29 eligibility for the exception by meeting at least one of the following criteria:
30 a. During the previous year, the covered entity and the providers and
31 entities on whose behalf it maintained, controlled, directed, or licensed
32 a data transfer system received in the aggregate less than one million
33 dollars ($1,000,000) in State health care funds for providing health
34 care services to beneficiaries of State-funded health care.
35 b. The covered entity and the providers and entities on whose behalf it
36 provides, maintains, controls, directs, or licenses a data transfer system
37 operated in whole or in part in a geographic area with limited or
38 emergent broadband availability. The Department of Information
39 Technology, Division of Broadband, shall identify these geographic
40 areas and the Authority shall publish a list of the identified geographic
41 areas to its website. Alternatively, the Authority, after consultation
42 with the Department of Information Technology, Division of
43 Broadband, may, in its discretion, grant a time-limited exception after
44 evaluating materials provided by a covered entity regarding its level
45 of broadband connectivity.
46 c. The covered entity will close, dissolve, or be acquired by another
47 entity within the next 12 months.
48 d. The provider or entity has not yet implemented or is in the process of
49 implementing a data transfer system.
50 (4) To request a time-limited exception under this subsection, the covered entity
51 shall submit to the Authority an application and attestation form, which shall
Page 166 Senate Bill 257-Second Edition
General Assembly Of North Carolina Session 2025
1 both be created by the Authority and made available on its website, containing
2 at least all of the following information:
3 a. Date of request and application period.

22 "§ 90-414.6. State ownership of HIE Network data.
23 Any data pertaining to services rendered to Medicaid and other beneficiaries of State-funded
24 health care program beneficiaries that is submitted through and stored by the HIE Network
25 pursuant to G.S. 90-414.4 or any other provision of this Article shall be and will remain the sole
26 property of the State. Any data or product derived from the aggregated, de-identified data
27 submitted to and stored by the HIE Network pursuant to G.S. 90-414.4 or any other provision of
28 this Article, shall be and will remain the sole property of the State. The Authority shall not allow
29 data it receives pursuant to G.S. 90-414.4 or any other provision of this Article to be used or
30 disclosed by or to any person or entity for commercial purposes or for any other purpose other
31 than those set forth in G.S. 90-414.4(a) or G.S. 90-414.2. To the extent the Authority receives
32 requests for electronic health information as the term is defined in 45 C.F.R. § 171.102, or other
33 medical records from an individual, an individual's personal representative, or an individual or
34 entity purporting to act on an individual's behalf, the Authority (i) shall not fulfill the request and
35 (ii) shall make available to the requester and the public, via the Authority's website, educational
36 materials about how to access such information from other sources. If the Authority participates
37 in the Trusted Exchange Framework and Common Agreement, then it may provide individual
38 access services through the Trusted Exchange Framework and Common Agreement. Patient
39 identifiers created and utilized by the Authority to integrate identity data in the HIE Network,
40 along with the minimum necessary required demographic information related to those patients,
41 shall be released to the GDAC and the Department by the Authority for purposes of entity
42 resolution and master data management. These identifiers shall not be considered public records
43 pursuant to Chapter 132 of the General Statutes.

13 (20) Adopt rules as needed to implement the appeal process established by
14 G.S. 90-414.15.

10 "§ 90-414.12. Penalties and remedies; immunity for covered entities and business associates
11 for good faith participation.
12 (a) Except as provided in subsection (b) of this section, a covered entity that discloses
13 protected health information in violation of this Article is subject to the following:
14 (1) Any civil penalty or criminal penalty, or both, that may be imposed on the
15 covered entity pursuant to the Health Information Technology for Economic
16 and Clinical Health (HITECH) Act, P.L. 111-5, Div. A, Title XIII, section
17 13001, as amended, and any regulations adopted under the HITECH
18 Act.federal law or regulation.
19 (2) Any civil remedy available under the HITECH Act or any regulations adopted
20 under the HITECH Act that is available to the Attorney General or to an
21 individual who has been harmed by a violation of this Article, including
22 damages, penalties, attorneys' fees, and costs.federal law or regulation.
23 (3) Disciplinary action by the respective licensing board or regulatory agency
24 with jurisdiction over the covered entity.
25 (4) Any penalty authorized under Article 2A of Chapter 75 of the General Statutes
26 if the violation of this Article is also a violation of Article 2A of Chapter 75
27 of the General Statutes.
28 (5) Any other civil or administrative remedy available to a plaintiff by State or
29 federal law or equity.
30 (a1) In connection with the submission of the annual compliance report required by
31 G.S. 90-414.13, it is unlawful for any person or entity to knowingly present or cause to be
32 presented to the Authority a false record to avoid full payment of the State health data assessment
33 under G.S. 90-414.4. The Authority may assess against any person or entity that violates this
34 subsection a civil penalty of not less than five thousand dollars ($5,000) and not more than ten
35 thousand dollars ($10,000), plus three times the amount of damages sustained by the Authority
36 as a result of that person's or entity's actions. The clear proceeds of civil penalties provided for
37 in this subsection shall be remitted to the Civil Penalty and Forfeiture Fund in accordance with
38 G.S. 115C-457.2.
39 (a2) The Authority may assess a civil penalty not to exceed fifty dollars ($50.00) for each
40 day after the required reporting period or deadline that the annual compliance report remains out
41 of compliance with the requirements prescribed by G.S. 90-414.13.
42 (b) To the extent permitted under or consistent with federal law, a covered entity or its
43 business associate that in good faith submits data through, accesses, uses, discloses, or relies
44 upon data submitted through the HIE Network shall not be subject to criminal prosecution or
45 civil liability for damages caused by such submission, access, use, disclosure, or reliance.
46 "§ 90-414.13. Annual compliance report.
47 (a) Reporting Requirement. – Each covered entity that provides, maintains, controls,
48 directs, or licenses the data transfer system of a provider or entity subject to G.S. 90-414.4(a1)
49 that provides health care services to beneficiaries of State-funded health care shall submit an
50 annual compliance report to the Authority on a form created by the Authority that meets the
51 requirements of this section.
Senate Bill 257-Second Edition Page 169
General Assembly Of North Carolina Session 2025
1 (b) The Authority shall develop and make available to covered entities an annual
2 compliance report form, which the Authority may update from time to time after consultation
3 with the Advisory Board. The annual compliance report form shall include fields for at least all
4 of the following information:
5 (1) Name of the covered entity, its location, and the Organization NPI.
6 (2) Names of providers and entities on whose behalf the covered entity is
7 submitting the annual compliance report, as well as their respective
8 Organization NPIs.
9 (3) Acknowledgment of the provision of health care services to beneficiaries of
10 State-funded health care.
11 (4) Status of technical connection to the HIE Network, as determined under
12 G.S. 90-414.4(a4).
13 (5) The status of data submission through the HIE Network that is in compliance
14 with G.S. 90-414.4.
15 (6) Representations regarding each of the following, as applicable:
16 a. For a covered entity that has executed an agreement with the
17 Authority, a representation regarding that entity's compliance with
18 such agreement.
19 b. For a covered entity that has received a time-limited exception from
20 the Authority, a representation regarding its present intent to connect
21 to, and begin submitting data through, the HIE Network.
22 c. For a covered entity that is required to pay the State health data
23 assessment fee authorized by G.S. 90-414.14, a representation
24 regarding the amount of the fee owed to the State, an explanation of
25 how the fee amount was calculated, and whether the fee was submitted
26 contemporaneously with the annual compliance report as required by
27 G.S. 90-414.14.
28 d. For a covered entity that asserts it is exempt from paying the annual
29 State health data assessment fee, representations regarding why it is
30 eligible to claim the exemption allowed by G.S. 90-414.14(e).
31 (7) Attestation to the completeness and validity of the annual compliance report
32 form and all representations contained on the form.
33 (c) Covered entities shall submit to the Authority all reports and related statements,
34 documents, and payments required by this section by the first of May each year. Covered entities
35 shall be deemed to have submitted timely annual compliance reports if complete reports are
36 postmarked or digitally time-stamped on or before the day the reports are due to the Authority.
37 If an annual compliance report or any related statements, documents, or payments are submitted
38 in a manner that does not comply with this section, the Authority may assess a civil penalty not
39 to exceed fifty dollars ($50.00) for each day after the first of May that the report remains out of
40 compliance with the requirements of this section. The clear proceeds of civil penalties provided
41 for in this subsection shall be remitted to the Civil Penalty and Forfeiture Fund in accordance
42 with G.S. 115C-457.2.
43 (d) A covered entity that provides, maintains, controls, directs, or licenses a data transfer
44 system solely on behalf of a provider or entity that voluntarily connects to the HIE Network
45 pursuant to G.S. 90-414.4(e) is not required to submit an annual compliance report.
46 (e) State agencies are required to submit an abbreviated annual compliance report, on a
47 form provided by the Authority, that shall be made available only to State agencies.
48 (f) At the request of a covered entity, the Authority may waive any requirement in this
49 section for good cause if the covered entity demonstrates to the satisfaction of the Authority that
50 complying with a requirement in this section would cause an undue hardship.

General Assembly Of North Carolina Session 2025
1 (g) The Department's Division of Health Benefits shall assist in administering the annual
2 compliance report process as it pertains to the State's Medicaid providers, as determined
3 necessary by the Authority. At a minimum, the Division of Health Benefits shall annually provide
4 the Authority with a current list of enrolled Medicaid providers, assist with notifying those
5 Medicaid providers about the annual compliance report requirement and reporting deadline
6 established by this section, and provide available information requested by the Authority that is
7 necessary for the Authority to audit or verify the completeness and accuracy of an enrolled
8 Medicaid provider's annual compliance report and related materials submitted to the Authority
9 by or on behalf of that provider.
10 "§ 90-414.14. Annual State health data assessment fee.
11 (a) Annual Fee Requirement. – Each covered entity that provides, maintains, controls,
12 directs, or licenses a data transfer system on behalf of a provider or entity subject to the
13 mandatory connection and data submission requirements of G.S. 90-414.4 shall pay an annual
14 State health data assessment fee each year if the covered entity meets any of the following
15 criteria:
16 (1) Is not connected to the HIE Network, as determined pursuant to subsection
17 (a4) of G.S. 90-414.4.
18 (2) Is connected to the HIE Network, as determined pursuant to subsection (a4)
19 of G.S. 90-414.4 but is not submitting required data through the HIE Network.
20 (b) Amount of Annual Fee. – The General Assembly shall determine the State health data
21 assessment fee schedules for annual compliance report periods.
22 (c) Fee Due Date. – A covered entity shall pay any required State health data assessment
23 fee contemporaneously with the submission of the annual compliance report required by
24 G.S. 90-414.13.
25 (d) HIE Network Data and Participation Fund; Use of Proceeds. – The HIE Network Data
26 and Participation Fund (Fund) is established as a special fund in the Department of Information
27 Technology under the management and control of the Authority. The Fund shall consist of the
28 fees collected by the Authority pursuant to this section and all other funds received by the
29 Authority pursuant to this Article, except for the clear proceeds of civil penalties collected
30 pursuant to G.S. 90-414.12, 90-414.13, 90-414.16, and subsection (g) of this section. The Fund
31 shall be placed in an interest-bearing account, and any interest or other income derived from the
32 Fund shall be credited to the Fund. The Authority shall not use monies in this Fund for any
33 purpose other than to pay for expenses incurred by the Authority in carrying out its powers and
34 duties as set forth in this Article. Monies in the Fund shall only be available for expenditure upon
35 an act of appropriation of the General Assembly. The Fund is subject to the provisions of the
36 State Budget Act, except that no unexpended surplus of the Fund shall revert to the General Fund.
37 (e) Fee Exemption. – A covered entity that provides, maintains, controls, directs, or
38 licenses a data transfer system for providers or entities subject to the HIE Network connection
39 and data submission requirements of this Article may claim an exemption from the State health
40 data assessment fee during a reporting period by demonstrating to the satisfaction of the
41 Authority that one or more of the following is true:
42 (1) The covered entity has secured a time-limited exception from the Authority
43 under G.S. 90-414.4(g) for the applicable State health data assessment fee
44 reporting period.
45 (2) The covered entity attests, in writing, that it and the providers and entities on
46 whose behalf it provides, maintains, controls, directs, or licenses a data
47 transfer system received less than five hundred thousand dollars ($500,000)
48 in State health care funds for providing health care services to beneficiaries of
49 State-funded health care.
50 (3) The covered entity is acting in good faith to comply with the Statewide Health
51 Information Exchange Act as evidenced by all of the following:
Senate Bill 257-Second Edition Page 171
General Assembly Of North Carolina Session 2025
1 a. Has entered into a participation agreement with the Authority.
2 b. Maintains contact with the Authority.
3 c. Timely responds to direct communications from the Authority
4 regarding matters such as connection status, onboarding, training, and
5 data submission.
6 (4) The covered entity is in its first year of existence, as evidenced by filings with
7 the Office of the Secretary of State.
8 (5) The covered entity attests, in writing, that it is actively transitioning between
9 data transfer systems.
10 (6) The covered entity is a prepaid health plan, as defined in G.S. 108D-1.
11 (f) Revocation of Exempt Status. – The Authority may revoke a covered entity's
12 exemption from payment of the State health data assessment fee if the covered entity is
13 unresponsive to communications from the Authority or if the covered entity fails to maintain
14 contact with the Authority. The Authority may revoke an exemption from the payment of the
15 State health data assessment fee for good cause after giving the covered entity 30 days' written
16 notice and an opportunity to cure any unresponsiveness to, or failure to maintain contact with,
17 the Authority.
18 (g) Civil Penalty for Submitting a False Record to Avoid the Fee. – It is unlawful for any
19 person or entity to knowingly present or cause to be presented to the Authority a false record to
20 avoid full payment of the State health data assessment fee due under this section. The Authority
21 shall assess against any person or entity that violates this section a civil penalty of not less than
22 five thousand dollars ($5,000) and not more than ten thousand dollars ($10,000), plus three times
23 the amount of damages sustained by the Authority as a result of that person's or entity's actions.
24 The clear proceeds of civil penalties provided for in this subsection shall be remitted to the Civil
25 Penalty and Forfeiture Fund in accordance with G.S. 115C-457.2.
26 "§ 90-414.15. Appeal of Authority's determinations.
27 (a) Determinations and Appeals. – The Authority shall make the following
28 determinations regarding providers' and entities' obligations: (i) grant or deny requests for
29 time-limited exceptions under G.S. 90-414.4 and (ii) assess penalties under G.S. 90-414.14. The
30 Authority shall send these determinations, in writing, to providers and entities via certified mail,
31 return receipt requested, and via email, if known to the Authority. If a provider or entity disagrees
32 with the Authority's determination, it shall deliver a petition for appeal to the Department of
33 Information Technology's registered agent via certified mail, return receipt requested, within 30
34 calendar days after receipt of the Authority's written determination. The petition for appeal shall
35 include an explanation of the specific reasons the provider or entity disagrees with the Authority's
36 determination and shall be supported by documentation and affidavits regarding the petitioner's
37 compliance with this Article along with any other supporting documentation the petitioner deems
38 relevant to the appeal. The Authority shall develop and make available on its website the form to
39 be used by any provider or entity seeking to appeal the Authority's determination.
40 (b) Untimely Appeals. – A petitioner's failure to submit a timely petition for appeal shall
41 result in the dismissal of the appeal with prejudice. The Department of Information Technology
42 shall notify the provider or entity of such dismissal in writing.
43 (c) Review by the State CIO or the State CIO's Designee. – The State CIO or the State
44 CIO's designee shall review all timely petitions for appeal under this section. The State CIO or
45 the State CIO's designee may render a decision on the petition without meeting with the
46 petitioner. If the State CIO or State CIO's designee renders a decision without meeting with the
47 petitioner, then the State CIO or the State CIO's designee shall notify the petitioner of his or her
48 decision, in writing, within 30 calendar days after the date the petition was received by the
49 Department of Information Technology. If the State CIO or the State CIO's designee determines
50 it is necessary to meet with the petitioner prior to rendering a decision, the State CIO or the State
51 CIO's designee and petitioner shall schedule a meeting within 30 calendar days after the date the
Page 172 Senate Bill 257-Second Edition
General Assembly Of North Carolina Session 2025
1 petition was received by the Department of Information Technology, or as soon as reasonably
2 practical thereafter, or as agreed upon by the parties. Within 30 calendar days after the date of
3 the meeting, the State CIO or the State CIO's designee shall submit a decision, in writing, to the
4 petitioner by certified mail, return receipt requested, and via email, if known.
5 (d) Administrative Review. – If the petitioner disagrees with the decision of the State
6 CIO or the State CIO's designee, the petitioner may commence a contested case under Article 3A
7 of Chapter 150B of the General Statutes. A petition for a contested case shall be filed within 30
8 calendar days after the earlier of either the date the decision of the State CIO or the State CIO's
9 designee is mailed to the petitioner or the date the decision of the State CIO or the State CIO's
10 designee is emailed to the petitioner. Except as otherwise provided by this Article, no other
11 disputes between the Authority and providers or entities, including disputes involving the terms
12 or conditions of any agreement described in G.S. 90-414.7(b), or a party's performance under
13 any such agreement, are subject to the contested case provisions of Chapter 150B of the General
14 Statutes.
15 "§ 90‑414.16. Data related services.
16 (a) Data Related Services. – The Authority may provide data related services to a covered
17 entity participating in the HIE Network or to a business associate of the participating covered
18 entity that is using the service to perform a function for the participating covered entity. Only
19 covered entities participating in the HIE Network may make a request to the Authority for data
20 related services. Nothing in this section shall be construed to require the Authority to provide
21 data related services to covered entities or their business associates. Data disclosed or used in the
22 Authority's provision of these services to any person or entity shall not be used for commercial
23 purposes.
24 (b) Cost Recovery. – If the Authority voluntarily elects to provide a data related service
25 to a covered entity, then it may charge a reasonable fee that may not exceed the actual cost
26 incurred for the service. The cost recovery shall be based on generally accepted accounting
27 principles and may include labor costs of the personnel providing the service, any information
28 technology expense, and any other administrative expense."

39 PART IX-C. CHILD AND FAMILY WELL-BEING [RESERVED]
40
41 PART IX-D. CHILD DEVELOPMENT AND EARLY EDUCATION
42
43 NC PRE-K PROGRAMS/STANDARDS FOR FOUR- AND FIVE-STAR RATED
44 FACILITIES
45 SECTION 9D.1.(a) Eligibility. – The Department of Health and Human Services,
46 Division of Child Development and Early Education, shall continue implementing the
47 prekindergarten program (NC Pre-K). The NC Pre-K program shall serve children who are 4
48 years of age on or before August 31 of the program year. In determining eligibility, the Division
49 shall establish income eligibility requirements for the program not to exceed seventy-five percent
50 (75%) of the State median income. Up to twenty percent (20%) of children enrolled may have
51 family incomes in excess of seventy-five percent (75%) of median income if those children have
Page 174 Senate Bill 257-Second Edition
General Assembly Of North Carolina Session 2025
1 other designated risk factors. Furthermore, any age-eligible child who is a child of either of the
2 following shall be eligible for the program: (i) an active duty member of the Armed Forces of the
3 United States, including the North Carolina National Guard, State military forces, or a reserve
4 component of the Armed Forces who was ordered to active duty by the proper authority within
5 the last 18 months or is expected to be ordered within the next 18 months, or (ii) a member of the
6 Armed Forces of the United States, including the North Carolina National Guard, State military
7 forces, or a reserve component of the Armed Forces who was injured or killed while serving on
8 active duty. Eligibility determinations for NC Pre-K participants may continue through local
9 education agencies and local North Carolina Partnership for Children, Inc., partnerships.
10 Other than developmental disabilities or other chronic health issues, the Division shall
11 not consider the health of a child as a factor in determining eligibility for participation in the NC
12 Pre-K program.
13 SECTION 9D.1.(a1) Staff-To-Child Ratio and Class Size. – The classroom shall not
14 exceed a maximum staff-to-child ratio of one to 10 with a maximum class size of 20 children,
15 with at least one teacher and one teacher assistant per classroom. A classroom of 10 children or
16 less shall have at least one teacher. The Child Care Commission shall adopt any rules and the
17 Division of Child Development and Early Education shall revise any rules or policies necessary
18 to implement the provisions of this subsection.
19 SECTION 9D.1.(b) Multiyear Contracts. – The Division of Child Development and
20 Early Education shall require the NC Pre-K contractor to issue multiyear contracts for licensed
21 private child care centers providing NC Pre-K classrooms.
22 SECTION 9D.1.(c) Building Standards. – Notwithstanding G.S. 110-91(4), private
23 child care facilities and public schools operating NC Pre-K classrooms shall meet the building
24 standards for preschool students as provided in G.S. 115C-521.1.
25 SECTION 9D.1.(d) Programmatic Standards. – Except as provided in subsection (c)
26 of this section, entities operating NC Pre-K classrooms shall adhere to all of the policies
27 prescribed by the Division of Child Development and Early Education regarding programmatic
28 standards and classroom requirements.
29 SECTION 9D.1.(e) NC Pre-K Committees. – Local NC Pre-K committees shall use
30 the standard decision-making process developed by the Division of Child Development and Early
31 Education in awarding NC Pre-K classroom slots and student selection.
32 SECTION 9D.1.(f) Reporting. – The Division of Child Development and Early
33 Education shall submit an annual report no later than March 15 of each year to the Joint
34 Legislative Oversight Committee on Health and Human Services, the Office of State Budget and
35 Management, and the Fiscal Research Division. The report shall include the following:
36 (1) The number of children participating in the NC Pre-K program by county.
37 (2) The number of children participating in the NC Pre-K program who have
38 never been served in other early education programs such as child care, public
39 or private preschool, Head Start, Early Head Start, or early intervention
40 programs.
41 (3) The expected NC Pre-K expenditures for the programs and the source of the
42 local contributions.
43 (4) The results of an annual evaluation of the NC Pre-K program.
44 SECTION 9D.1.(g) Audits. – The administration of the NC Pre-K program by local
45 partnerships shall be subject to the financial and compliance audits authorized under
46 G.S. 143B-168.14(b).
47
48 NC PRE-K/REPORT ON REALLOCATION OF UNUSED SLOTS
49 SECTION 9D.2. The Department of Health and Human Services, Division of Child
50 Development and Early Education (Division), shall submit a report to the Joint Legislative
51 Oversight Committee on Health and Human Services and the Fiscal Research Division by March
Senate Bill 257-Second Edition Page 175
General Assembly Of North Carolina Session 2025
1 1, 2026, on how unused slots are reallocated in the NC Prekindergarten (NC Pre-K) program.
2 The report shall include, at a minimum, the following:
3 (1) A description of the number of unused slots following the 2022-2023 program
4 year.
5 (2) Options for changes to the administration of the program that would allow
6 unused slots to be used by counties that have waiting lists of eligible children
7 and sufficient providers to use those slots that program year.
8 (3) Any other information the Division deems relevant to the issue of chronically
9 unused NC Pre-K slots.
