8 ALL STATE-SUPPORTED PERSONNEL
9 SECTION 41.20.(a) The legislative salary increases authorized by this act shall be
10 paid effective on July 1, 2025, and do not apply to persons separated from service due to
11 resignation, dismissal, reduction in force, death, or retirement or whose last workday is prior to
12 June 30, 2025.
13 SECTION 41.20.(b) The Director of the Budget is granted flexibility to administer
14 the compensation increases enacted by this act. The State employer contribution rates enacted by
15 this act for retirement and related benefits may be deemed by the Director of the Budget for
16 administrative purposes to become effective after July 1 to provide flexibility in the collection
17 and reconciliation of salary-related contributions as required by law, provided the estimated
18 amount contributed to any affected employee benefit trust equals the amount that would have
19 been contributed to the employee benefit trust if the enacted employer contribution rates had
20 been effective on July 1.
21 SECTION 41.20.(c) This section applies to all employees paid from State funds,
22 whether or not subject to or exempt from the North Carolina Human Resources Act, including
23 employees of public schools, community colleges, and The University of North Carolina.
24
25 USE OF FUNDS APPROPRIATED FOR LEGISLATIVELY MANDATED INCREASES
26 SECTION 41.21.(a) The Office of State Budget and Management shall ensure that
27 the appropriations made by this act for legislatively mandated salary increases and employee
28 benefits are used only for those purposes.
29 SECTION 41.21.(b) If the Director of the Budget determines that funds appropriated
30 to a State agency for legislatively mandated salary increases and employee benefits exceed the
31 amount required by that agency for those purposes, the Director may reallocate those funds to
32 other State agencies that received insufficient funds for legislatively mandated salary increases
33 and employee benefits.
34 SECTION 41.21.(c) Funds appropriated for legislatively mandated salary and
35 employee benefit increases may not be used to adjust the budgeted salaries of vacant positions,
36 to provide salary increases in excess of those required by the General Assembly, or to increase
37 the budgeted salary of filled positions to the minimum of the position's respective salary range.
38 SECTION 41.21.(d) Any funds appropriated for legislatively mandated salary and
39 employee benefit increases in excess of the amounts required to implement the increases shall be
40 credited to the Pay Plan Reserve.
41 SECTION 41.21.(e) No later than May 1, 2026, the Office of State Budget and
42 Management shall report to the Fiscal Research Division on the expenditure of funds for
43 legislatively mandated salary increases and employee benefits. This report shall include at least
44 the following information for each State agency:
45 (1) The total amount of funds that the agency received for legislatively mandated
46 salary increases and employee benefits.
47 (2) The total amount of funds transferred from the agency to other State agencies
48 pursuant to subsection (b) of this section. This section of the report shall
49 identify the amounts transferred to each recipient State agency.
50 (3) The total amount of funds used by the agency for legislatively mandated salary
51 increases and employee benefits.
Page 390 Senate Bill 257-Second Edition

General Assembly Of North Carolina Session 2025
1 (4) The amount of funds credited to the Pay Plan Reserve.

3 SALARY-RELATED CONTRIBUTIONS
4 SECTION 41.22.(a) Effective for the 2025-2027 fiscal biennium, required employer
5 salary-related contributions for employees whose salaries are paid from department, office,
6 institution, or agency receipts shall be paid from the same source as the source of the employee's
7 salary. If an employee's salary is paid in part from the General Fund or Highway Fund and in
8 part from department, office, institution, or agency receipts, required employer salary-related
9 contributions may be paid from the General Fund or Highway Fund only to the extent of the
10 proportionate part paid from the General Fund or Highway Fund in support of the salary of the
11 employee, and the remainder of the employer's requirements shall be paid from the source that
12 supplies the remainder of the employee's salary. The requirements of this section as to source of
13 payment are also applicable to payments on behalf of the employee for hospital medical benefits,
14 longevity pay, unemployment compensation, accumulated leave, workers' compensation,
15 severance pay, separation allowances, and applicable disability income benefits.

34 SECTION 41.23.(b) Article 86 of Chapter 58 of the General Statutes is amended by
35 adding a new section to read:
36 "§ 58-86-41. Amount due for membership; payments credited to separate member
37 accounts.
38 (a) Unless otherwise provided under this Article, each member of the Pension Fund shall
39 pay the sum of fifteen dollars ($15.00) per month to the Pension Fund for membership in the
40 fund for a period not to exceed 20 years.
41 (b) Unless otherwise provided under this Article, all payments due in any calendar year
42 shall be made no later than March 31 subsequent to the end of the calendar year in which the
43 payment was due.
44 (c) The Pension Fund shall not award fully credited service based on payments received
45 later than March 31 subsequent to the end of the calendar year in which the month occurred
46 unless the payment is applied as provided in G.S. 58-86-45(a1).
47 (d) Payments made in accordance with this section shall be credited to the separate
48 account of the member and shall be kept by the custodian in a manner that allows the payments
49 to be made available upon a member's withdrawal from membership or retirement."

33 INCREASE BENEFITS PAYABLE UNDER NORTH CAROLINA NATIONAL GUARD
34 PENSION FUND
35 SECTION 41.24.(a) G.S. 127A-40 reads as rewritten:
36 "§ 127A-40. Pensions for the members of the North Carolina National Guard.
37 (a) Every member and former member of the North Carolina National Guard who meets
38 the requirements of this section shall receive, commencing at age 60, a pension of one hundred
39 five eight dollars ($105.00) ($108.00) per month for 20 years' creditable military service with an
40 additional ten dollars and fifty cents ($10.50) eighty cents ($10.80) per month for each additional
41 year of such creditable military service; provided, however, that the total pension shall not exceed
42 two hundred ten sixteen dollars ($210.00) ($216.00) per month. The requirements for
43 (a1) To receive a pension are that under this section, each member shall:shall meet all of
44 the following requirements:

4 SECTION 41.24.(b) This section applies to pension benefit amounts payable from

25 service. G.S. 135-5(a). The allowance shall be paid in equal installments on the payroll frequency

General Assembly Of North Carolina Session 2025
1 (b1) Eligibility for Allowance and Calculation of Allowance Amount. – To be eligible for
2 an allowance under this section, an officer is required to meet one of the following sets of criteria
3 that shall also determine the allowance amount:
4 (1) For officers meeting all of the following criteria, the annual special separation
5 allowance to be paid is equal to eighty-five hundredths percent (0.85%) of the
6 annual base rate of compensation most recently applicable to the officer for
7 each year of that officer's creditable service:
8 a. The officer (i) has completed 30 or more years of creditable service or
9 (ii) is 55 years of age or older and completed five or more years of
10 creditable service.
11 b. The officer is less than 62 years of age.
12 c. The officer has completed at least five years of continuous service as
13 a law enforcement officer immediately preceding the officer's service
14 retirement. Any break in this required continuous service that is a
15 result of disability retirement or disability salary continuation benefits
16 shall not adversely affect an officer's qualification to receive an
17 allowance under this subdivision so long as the officer returned to
18 service within 45 days after the disability benefits had ceased and is
19 otherwise qualified to receive the allowance.
20 d. At least fifty percent (50%) of the officer's creditable service is as a
21 law enforcement officer, or for service prior to July 1, 2017, as a
22 probation/parole officer.
23 (2) For officers meeting all of the following criteria, the annual special separation
24 allowance to be paid is equal to eighty-five hundredths percent (0.85%) of the
25 annual equivalent of the base rate of compensation at the time the officer
26 attained 30 years of service multiplied by 30:
27 a. Prior to attaining 62 years of age, the officer has completed 30 or more
28 years of creditable service, at least fifty percent (50%) of which was
29 as a law enforcement officer, or for service prior to July 1, 2017, as a
30 probation/parole officer.
31 b. The officer has completed at least five years of continuous service as
32 a law enforcement officer immediately preceding the officer's service
33 retirement. Any break in this required continuous service that is a
34 result of disability retirement or disability salary continuation benefits
35 shall not adversely affect an officer's qualification to receive an
36 allowance under this subdivision so long as the officer returned to
37 service within 45 days after the disability benefits had ceased and is
38 otherwise qualified to receive the allowance.
39 If an officer meets all of the criteria under each subdivision of this subsection, then the
40 employer making the allowance payments shall allow the officer to choose which of the two
41 calculation formulas to use for that officer's allowance. This election by the officer is a one-time,
42 irrevocable election and shall be made prior to the first allowance payment. If no election is made
43 by the officer, then the calculation amount under subdivision (2) of this subsection shall be used.

45 provisions of this section shall cease at the first of:occurrence of one of the following:

34 INCREASE THE AMOUNT AWARDED FOR LINE OF DUTY DEATHS UNDER THE
35 PUBLIC SAFETY EMPLOYEES' DEATH BENEFITS ACT
36 SECTION 41.26.(a) G.S. 143-166.3 reads as rewritten:
37 "§ 143-166.3. Payments; determination.
38 (a) When any covered person is killed in the line of duty, the Industrial Commission shall
39 award a death benefit in the amount of one hundred thousand dollars ($100,000) one hundred
40 fifty thousand dollars ($150,000) to be paid to one of the following:
41 (1) The spouse of the covered person if there is a surviving spouse.
42 (2) If there is no surviving spouse, then payments shall be made to any surviving
43 dependent child of the covered person. If there is more than one surviving
44 dependent child, then the payment shall be made to and equally divided among
45 all surviving dependent children.
46 (3) If there is no surviving spouse and no surviving dependent child or children,
47 then payments shall be made to any surviving dependent parent of the covered
48 person. If there is more than one surviving dependent parent, then the
49 payments shall be made to and equally divided between the surviving
50 dependent parents of the covered person.
Senate Bill 257-Second Edition Page 401

General Assembly Of North Carolina Session 2025
1 (4) If there is no surviving spouse, surviving dependent child, or surviving
2 dependent parent, then the payment shall be made to the estate of the deceased
3 covered person.
4 …
5 (e) On and after July 1, 2016, when When any covered person is murdered in the line of
6 duty, in addition to the award under subsection (a) of this section, the Industrial Commission
7 shall award a death benefit in the amount of one hundred thousand dollars ($100,000) one
8 hundred fifty thousand dollars ($150,000) to be paid to one of the following:
9 (1) The spouse of the covered person if there is a surviving spouse.
10 (2) If there is no surviving spouse, then payments shall be made to any surviving
11 dependent child of the covered person. If there is more than one surviving
12 dependent child, then the payment shall be made to and equally divided among
13 all surviving dependent children.
14 (3) If there is no surviving spouse and no surviving dependent child or children,
15 then payments shall be made to any surviving dependent parent of the covered
16 person. If there is more than one surviving dependent parent, then the
17 payments shall be made to and equally divided between the surviving
18 dependent parents of the covered person.
19 (4) If there is no surviving spouse, surviving dependent child, or surviving
20 dependent parent, then the payment shall be made to the estate of the deceased
21 covered person.
22 …."
23 SECTION 41.26.(b) This section is effective July 1, 2025, and applies to deaths that
24 occur on or after that date.

18 SECTION 42.5.(b) No later than June 1, 2027, and every two years thereafter until
19 project completion, the Department shall report on the use of these funds to the Joint Legislative
20 Commission on Governmental Operations, the Fiscal Research Division, and the Office of State
21 Budget and Management. Each report shall include all of the following:
22 (1) The status of all projects undertaken pursuant to this section.
23 (2) The estimated total cost of each project.
24 (3) The date that work on each project began or is expected to begin.
25 (4) The date that work on each project was completed or is expected to be
26 completed.
27 (5) The actual cost of each project, including federal matching funds.
28 (6) Facilities planned for closure or reversion.
29 (7) A list of projects advanced in schedule, those projects delayed in schedule,
30 and an estimate of the amount of funds expected to revert to the General Fund.

32 GRANTS TO NON-STATE ENTITIES
33 SECTION 42.6. Requirements. – For purposes of this Part, nonrecurring funds
34 allocated from the State Capital and Infrastructure Fund as grants to non-State entities, as defined
35 by G.S. 143C-1-1(d), are subject to all of the following requirements:
36 (1) As soon as practicable after the effective date of this act, each State agency
37 administering grants shall begin disbursement of funds to each grantee
38 non-State entity when all applicable requirements are met. However,
39 disbursement of grant funds allocated for the 2025-2026 fiscal year shall
40 commence no later than 100 days after the date this act becomes law, and
41 disbursement in full to all grantees shall be completed no later than nine
42 months after the date this act becomes law.
43 (2) G.S. 143C-6-23(b) through (f) and (f2) through (k) apply to the grants.
44 (3) Notwithstanding any provision of G.S. 143C-1-2(b) to the contrary, unless
45 otherwise indicated, nonrecurring funds appropriated in this Part as grants
46 shall not revert until expended or the particular project has been completed.
47 (4) Grants to each grantee non-State entity shall be used for nonsectarian,
48 nonreligious purposes only.
49 (5) By January 1, 2026, and then quarterly thereafter, the Office of State Budget
50 and Management shall report to the Fiscal Research Division on the schedule
Senate Bill 257-Second Edition Page 409

General Assembly Of North Carolina Session 2025
1 for and status of grant disbursement. At a minimum, the report shall include
2 the following for each grant:
3 a. The date when the disbursing agency issued the initial contract.
4 b. The date when the contract was sent to the grantee non-State entity.
5 c. The date when the fully executed contract was returned to the
6 disbursing agency.

10 DOWNTOWN GOVERNMENT COMPLEX
11 SECTION 42.7.(a) The Department of Administration shall sell the property situated
12 on the parcel of land in the City of Raleigh, with Wake County real estate ID# 0179265,
13 commonly known as 304 N. Dawson Street, for fair market value. No service charge into the
14 State Land Fund shall be deducted from or levied against the proceeds of the sale of the property
15 listed in this subsection. Notwithstanding G.S. 146-30, the proceeds of the sale of the property
16 listed in this subsection shall be handled in accordance with the following priority:
17 (1) First, in accordance with the provisions of any trust or other instrument of title
18 whereby title to the subject real property was acquired by the State.
19 (2) Second, to reimburse the Department of Administration for any funds
20 expended in the sale of the subject real property.
21 (3) Third, to be deposited into the State Capital and Infrastructure Fund,
22 established in G.S. 143C-4-3.1.
23 The Department of Administration shall obtain an appraisal assessing the value for
24 the property listed in this subsection according to their best and highest use and shall submit the
25 appraisal to the Joint Legislative Oversight Committee on Capital Improvements and the Fiscal
26 Research Division no later than January 1, 2026.
27 SECTION 42.7.(b) The Department of Administration shall prepare a plan that,
28 within 18 months of the effective date of this section, would consolidate and move the offices of
29 the State Records Center and any storage or satellite facilities related to the State Records Center
30 to another location outside of the downtown government complex. The Department of
31 Administration shall consider options for lease or purchase and shall submit its plan and cost
32 estimates to the Joint Legislative Oversight Committee on Capital Improvements and the Fiscal
33 Research Division no later than March 1, 2026.

27 TRANSPORTATION DISASTER RECOVERY FOR HURRICANE HELENE
28 SECTION 43.3.(a) Clawback DMV IT Modernization Funds. – Whereas the
29 General Assembly tasked the Division of Motor Vehicles (DMV) with service and information
30 technology modernization efforts in 2013, and has appropriated over one hundred fifty million
31 dollars ($150,000,000) for this purpose, and since there still remains a cash balance of more than
32 one hundred million dollars ($100,000,000), which continues to accumulate, now therefore,
33 notwithstanding any other provision of law, of the funds previously appropriated to the Division
34 of Motor Vehicles of the Department of Transportation for information technology
35 modernization projects and available in NCFS Budget Fund 801169 or NCDOT SAP Fund Code
36 150021, the sum of sixty-five million dollars ($65,000,000) shall be included under adjustments
37 to availability for the Highway Fund in Section 3.2 of this act.
38 SECTION 43.3.(b) Clawback Remaining Dorian Funds. – Whereas the General
39 Assembly appropriated thirty million dollars ($30,000,000) for debris removal and repair of
40 highway infrastructure damage from Hurricane Dorian in 2019, and since all debris removal and
41 infrastructure repair from this storm have been completed and there remains a cash balance of
42 more than twenty-eight million dollars ($28,000,000) in unexpended funds, now therefore,
43 notwithstanding any other provision of law, of the remaining funds previously appropriated to
44 the Department of Transportation from the General Fund for Hurricane Dorian recovery available
45 in NCFS Budget Fund 801278, the sum of twenty-eight million one hundred sixty-one thousand
46 four hundred eighteen dollars ($28,161,418) shall be included under adjustments to availability
47 for the Highway Fund in Section 3.2 of this act.
48 SECTION 43.3.(c) Recovery Funds. – The following amounts from the Reserve for
49 General Maintenance (GMR) and from the unreserved credit balance to the GMR (Credit Balance
50 Funds) shall be used for cash flow and federal matching purposes for activities related to recovery
51 from Hurricane Helene:
Senate Bill 257-Second Edition Page 411

General Assembly Of North Carolina Session 2025
1 (1) Notwithstanding subsections (f) and (f1) of G.S. 136-44.2 and any other
2 provision of law to the contrary, for the 2025-2026 fiscal year the Department
3 of Transportation shall use up to two hundred seventy million dollars
4 ($270,000,000) in Credit Balance Funds.
5 (2) Notwithstanding subsections (f) and (f1) of G.S. 136-44.2 and any other
6 provision of law to the contrary, the Department of Transportation shall use
7 all future Credit Balance Funds until recovery is completed.
8 (3) Of the nonrecurring funds appropriated to GMR for the 2025-2027 fiscal
9 biennium, the sum of two hundred forty-seven million three hundred
10 sixty-nine thousand six hundred thirty dollars ($247,369,630) in nonrecurring
11 funds for the 2025-2026 fiscal year and the sum of one hundred sixteen million
12 dollars ($116,000,000) in nonrecurring funds for the 2026-2027 fiscal year.
13 SECTION 43.3.(d) Management of Funds. – All funds used for Hurricane Helene
14 recovery as set forth in this section shall be managed in a separate project account within GMR.
15 Federal reimbursements for funds expended from this account shall be deposited in this account.
16 Upon recovery completion, any remaining Recovery Funds shall be transferred to GMR for use
17 by the Department.
18 SECTION 43.3.(e) Cash Watch Weekly Report. – In addition to the other items
19 published in the weekly report required under G.S. 143C-6-11(n), the Department shall include
20 the total sum of Hurricane Helene expenditures and the total sum of federal reimbursements
21 received by the Department. This requirement shall remain in effect until recovery is complete
22 and the Department has received all federal reimbursements.

24 DISASTER REIMBURSEMENT REPORTS
25 SECTION 43.4. Article 2A of Chapter 136 of the General Statutes is amended by
26 adding a new section to read:
27 "§ 136-44.2F. Disaster reimbursement reports.
28 (a) Disaster Detailed Report. – No later than the end of each month, the Department of
29 Transportation shall submit a report to the Joint Legislative Transportation Oversight Committee
30 and the Fiscal Research Division on disaster expenditures that qualify for federal reimbursement.
31 The report shall be categorized by disaster and include the following information:
32 (1) Project number.
33 (2) Project description.
34 (3) Highway division.
35 (4) County.
36 (5) Total project expenditures to date.
37 (6) Federal disaster program eligibility.
38 (7) Estimated expenditures eligible for reimbursement.
39 (8) Date of initial reimbursement submission.
40 (9) Date of last reimbursement submission.
41 (10) Eligible expenditures submitted for reimbursement.
42 (11) Anticipated reimbursement.
43 (12) An explanation if the anticipated amount of reimbursement is less than the
44 estimate of expenditures eligible for reimbursement.
45 (13) Reimbursements received to date.
46 (b) Disaster Summary Report. – No later than the end of each quarter, the Department
47 shall submit a summary report to the Joint Legislative Transportation Oversight Committee and
48 the Fiscal Research Division for all disaster expenditures resulting from a disaster that occurred
49 on or after January 1, 2016, and that qualify for federal reimbursement. The report shall be by
50 disaster and contain the source of federal reimbursement and the total eligible expenditures as of
51 the date of the report.
Page 412 Senate Bill 257-Second Edition

General Assembly Of North Carolina Session 2025
1 (c) Failure to Submit Report. – If the Department fails to submit a report under this
2 section within 60 days of the required submission date, the Secretary of the Department shall
3 provide to the Joint Legislative Commission on Governmental Operations and the Fiscal
4 Research Division an explanation for not submitting the required report."

28 CAPITAL INFRASTRUCTURE PLAN

33 Projects. The plan shall include examining the disposal of unused and underutilized real property
34 of the Department to fund this plan. The Department shall submit the plan to the Joint Legislative
35 Transportation Oversight Committee and the Fiscal Research Division by March 15, 2026.

37 ESTABLISH BOARD OF MOTOR VEHICLES
38 SECTION 43.9.(a) G.S. 20-2(a) reads as rewritten:
39 "(a) Commissioner and Assistants. – The Division of Motor Vehicles shall be
40 administered by the Commissioner of Motor Vehicles, who shall be appointed by and serve at
41 the pleasure of the Secretary of the Department of Transportation. Board of Motor Vehicles. The
42 Commissioner shall be paid an annual salary to be fixed by the Governor and allowed traveling
43 expenses as allowed by law.
44 In any action, proceeding, or matter of any kind, to which the Commissioner of Motor
45 Vehicles is a party or in which he may have an interest, all pleadings, legal notices, proof of
46 claim, warrants for collection, certificates of tax liability, executions, and other legal documents,
47 may be signed and verified on behalf of the Commissioner of Motor Vehicles by the Assistant
48 Commissioner of Motor Vehicles or by any director or assistant director of any section of the
49 Division of Motor Vehicles or by any other agent or employee of the Division so authorized by
50 the Commissioner of Motor Vehicles."
Senate Bill 257-Second Edition Page 413

General Assembly Of North Carolina Session 2025
1 SECTION 43.9.(b) Article 1 of Chapter 20 of the General Statutes is amended by
2 adding a new section to read:
3 "§ 20-2.1. Board of Motor Vehicles.
4 (a) There is hereby created a Board of Motor Vehicles. The Board is responsible for
5 appointing the Commissioner of Motor Vehicles and overseeing the organization and
6 administration of the Division of Motor Vehicles by the Commissioner.
7 (b) The Board consists of nine voting members as follows:
8 (1) The Secretary of Transportation or the Secretary's designee.
9 (2) The Secretary of the Department of Information Technology or the Secretary's
10 designee.
11 (3) The Commander of the State Highway Patrol or the Commander's designee.
12 (4) Two members appointed by the Governor.
13 (5) Two members appointed by the General Assembly upon recommendation of
14 the President Pro Tempore of the Senate.
15 (6) Two members appointed by the General Assembly upon recommendation of
16 the Speaker of the House of Representatives.
17 (c) The terms of one member of the Board each appointed under subdivisions (4), (5),
18 and (6) of subsection (b) of this section shall begin on July 1, 2025, and expire on June 30, 2027.
19 The terms of one member of the Board each appointed under subdivisions (4), (5), and (6) of
20 subsection (b) of this section shall begin on July 1, 2025, and expire on June 30, 2029. Thereafter,
21 all Board members appointed under subdivisions (4) through (6) of subsection (b) of this section
22 shall be appointed for four-year terms beginning on July 1 of the year that the previous term
23 expired.
24 (d) Board members appointed under subdivisions (4) through (6) of subsection (b) of this
25 section shall continue to serve until their successors are appointed. The appointing authority may
26 appoint a member to serve out the unexpired term of a Board member appointed by that
27 appointing authority. The appointing authority may remove a member of the Board appointed by
28 that appointing authority for any cause the appointing authority finds sufficient.
29 (e) The Board shall meet at least quarterly and may meet at any time at the call of the
30 chairperson or any three members of the Board. All clerical and other services required by the
31 Board shall be supplied by the Secretary of the Department of Transportation.
32 (f) The Board shall select a chairperson and may select other officers from among its
33 membership. The Board shall have the power to adopt and enforce rules and regulations for the
34 government of its business and proceedings. A majority of the Board shall constitute a quorum
35 for the transaction of business. Board members shall receive per diem and necessary travel and
36 subsistence expenses in accordance with G.S. 138-5 and G.S. 138-6, as appropriate."

40 Transportation, shall organize and administer the Division in such manner as he the
41 Commissioner may deem necessary to conduct the work of the Division."

43 ELIMINATE MOTOR VEHICLE REGISTRATION CARDS AND STICKERS
44 SECTION 43.10.(a) It is the intent of the General Assembly to reduce administrative
45 costs, streamline vehicle registration processes, and enhance efficiency by eliminating motor
46 vehicle registration cards and motor vehicle registration renewal stickers.
47 SECTION 43.10.(b) The Division of Motor Vehicles of the Department of
48 Transportation shall develop, implement, and maintain a secure and user-friendly electronic
49 vehicle registration system that allows vehicle owners, law enforcement agencies, and other
50 authorized entities to access and verify registration status without the need for physical
51 registration cards or renewal stickers.

General Assembly Of North Carolina Session 2025

15 contractors based on their performance.

18 qualified contractor prior to expiration of the contract. A qualified contractor is a person, firm,
19 corporation, or governmental subdivision of the State of North Carolina, with demonstrated
20 experience as a commission contractor in North Carolina or equivalent experience in another
21 state, as determined by the Division. All Division equipment and software shall be transferred to

23 Division.

9 SECTION 43.10.(o) G.S. 20-67 reads as rewritten:
10 "§ 20-67. Notice of change of address or name.
11 (a) Address. – A person whose address changes from the address stated on a certificate
12 of title or registration card electronic notice of registration must notify the Division of the change
13 within 60 days after the change occurs. The person may obtain a duplicate certificate of title or
14 registration card stating the new address but is not required to do so. The Division shall update
15 the person's electronic record of registration. A person who does not move but whose address
16 changes due to governmental action may not be charged with violating this subsection.
17 (b) Name. – A person whose name changes from the name stated on a certificate of title
18 or registration card electronic notice of registration must notify the Division of the change within
19 60 days after the change occurs. The person may obtain a duplicate certificate of title or
20 registration card but is not required to do so.
21 (c) Fee. – G.S. 20-85 sets the fee for a duplicate certificate of title or registration card."

11 thereto is unable to present a certificate of title thereto by reason of the same being lost or
12 unlawfully detained by one in possession, or the same is otherwise not available, the Division is
13 hereby authorized to receive such application and to examine into the circumstances of the case,
14 and may require the filing of affidavits or other information; and when the Division is satisfied
15 that the applicant is entitled thereto and that G.S. 20-72 has been complied with, it is hereby
16 authorized to to: (i) register such vehicle that vehicle, (ii) update the electronic record of
17 registration associated with that vehicle, and (iii) issue a new registration card, electronic notice
18 of registration, registration plate or plates and certificates of title to the person entitled thereto,
19 upon payment of proper fees.
20 …
21 (c) Whenever an applicant for the registration of a moped is unable to present a
22 manufacturer's certificate of origin for the moped, the applicant must submit an affidavit stating
23 why the applicant does not have the manufacturer's certificate of origin and attesting that the
24 applicant is entitled to registration. Upon receipt of the application and accompanying affidavit,
25 the Division shall shall: (i) update the electronic record of registration associated with the moped
26 and (ii) issue the applicant a registration card an electronic notice of registration and a registration
27 plate. The Division may not require the applicant to post a bond as required under subsection (b)
28 of this section. A person damaged by issuance of the registration card an update to an electronic
29 record of registration or the issuance of an electronic notice of registration does not have a right
30 of action against the Division."

43 a period of 20 years. After 20 years, the Division shall maintain a record of the last two owners.

24 SECTION 43.10.(ii) G.S. 105-330.4 reads as rewritten:
25 "§ 105-330.4. Due date, interest, and enforcement remedies.
26 (a) Due Date. – The registration of a classified motor vehicle may not be issued unless a
27 temporary registration plate is issued for the motor vehicle under G.S. 20-79.1A or the taxes for
28 the motor vehicle's tax year that begins after the issuance of the registration are paid upon
29 registration. A registration of a classified motor vehicle may not be renewed unless the taxes for
30 the motor vehicle's tax year that begins after the registration expires are paid upon registration.
31 If the registration of a classified motor vehicle is renewed earlier than the date the taxes are due,
32 the taxes must be paid as if they were due. Taxes on a classified motor vehicle are due as follows:
33 (1) For an unregistered classified motor vehicle, the taxes are due on September
34 1 following the date by which the vehicle was required to be listed.
35 (2) For a registered classified motor vehicle that is registered under the staggered
36 system, the taxes are due each year on the date the owner applies for a new
37 registration or the fifteenth day of the month following the month in which the
38 registration renewal sticker expires pursuant to G.S. 20-66(g).
39 (3) For a registered classified motor vehicle that is registered under the annual
40 system, taxes are due on the date the owner applies for a new registration or
41 45 days after the registration expires.
42 (4) For a registered classified motor vehicle that has a temporary registration plate
43 issued under G.S. 20-79.1 or a limited registration plate issued under
44 G.S. 20-79.1A, the taxes are due on the last day of the second month following
45 the date the owner applied for the plate.
46 …."

34 NORTH CAROLINA RAILROAD DIVIDEND
35 SECTION 43.16. G.S. 124-18, as it existed before it was repealed by Section 4.4(b)
36 of S.L. 2019-231, is reenacted and reads as rewritten:
37 "§ 124-18. Dividends required of State-owned railroad company.
38 Any State-owned railroad company that has trackage in more than two counties shall issue
39 an annual cash dividend to the State. The dividend shall be deposited in the Freight Rail & Rail
40 Crossing Safety Improvement Fund within the Highway Fund. The amount of the annual
41 dividend is twenty-five percent (25%) of the company's income from the prior year's trackage
42 rights agreements. The dividend is due by February 15 of each year, and interest shall accrue at
43 the annual rate of prime plus one percent (1%) if the payment is not paid by the due date. The
44 Directors of any State-owned railroad company who vote for or assent to the dividend required
45 under this section shall not be held liable under G.S. 55-8-33."

1 (a) Subject to subsection (c) of this section, in order to minimize the amount of just
2 compensation due, whenever property on which a lawfully erected outdoor advertising sign is
3 located is acquired by a public or private condemnor, as defined in G.S. 40A-3, or the Department
4 of Transportation, and the acquiring party requires removal of the sign, or whenever the
5 construction of a sound barrier wall would impair the visibility of a lawfully erected outdoor
6 advertising sign, the eligible sign is permitted to be relocated and reconstructed, subject to all of
7 the following requirements:reconstructed. For relocated signs that are subject to the jurisdiction
8 of the Department of Transportation in accordance with this Article, the follow relocation and
9 reconstruction conditions shall apply:
10 (1) The new site for relocation is permitted to be any area within 660 feet of the
11 nearest edge of the right-of-way of a highway on the National System of
12 Interstate and Defense Highways or the federal aid primary highway system
13 within the same zoning jurisdiction as the relocated site or, if the relocated site
14 is within an unzoned city or county, then within the same territorial limits.
15 (2) The new site for relocation must be conforming to State standards as set forth
16 in this Article and pursuant to rules and regulations promulgated by the
17 Department as authorized by this Article.
18 (3) The new site for relocation must be along a highway on the interstate system
19 or primary systems or other State road that has the same route number and
20 letter or one of the same route numbers and letters as the highway or State
21 road adjacent to the relocated site.
22 (4) The reconstruction of the outdoor advertising sign at the new site shall comply
23 with G.S. 136-131.2.
24 (5) The new site for relocation shall not be within an historic district lawfully
25 established by a local city or county government pursuant to Part 4 of Article
26 9 of Chapter 160D of the General Statutes, unless consented to by a resolution
27 adopted by the applicable local governing board.
28 (6) The new site for relocation shall not be adjacent to any scenic highway as
29 provided in G.S. 136-129.2; provided, however, if a relocated sign is already
30 adjacent to a scenic highway, it may be relocated within the same parcel.
31 (7) The construction work related to the relocation of the outdoor advertising sign
32 shall commence within one year after the date of removal.
33 The express allowances of relocation and reconstruction in this section shall apply to any
34 lawfully erected outdoor advertising sign anywhere within this State that is required to be
35 removed as a result of action taken by a public or private condemnor, as defined in G.S. 40A-3,
36 or the Department of Transportation, including such signs that are any billboard that is not subject
37 to the jurisdiction of the Department of Transportation.located along a State road so long as the
38 affected sign is relocated and reconstructed within the same parcel or, if outside the parcel, to a
39 commercially or industrially zoned property or, if unzoned, a commercially or industrially used
40 property, and within the same territorial limits and the sign owner complies with subdivisions (3)
41 through (7) of this subsection, except that a Department permit is not required to be in effect.
42 (b) Subject to subsection (c) of this section, any outdoor advertising sign that does not
43 otherwise qualify for relocation as provided in subsection (a) of this section and for which there
44 is in effect a valid permit issued by the Department of Transportation pursuant to this Article is
45 permitted to be relocated and reconstructed subject to all of the requirements listed in
46 subdivisions (1) through (7) of subsection (a) of this section within the same parcel or an
47 adjoining conforming parcel. No sign shall be relocated pursuant to this subsection within 10
48 years from the date of the last relocation pursuant to this subsection, however, this temporal
49 limitation does not apply to relocations within the same parcel. For purposes of this Section, the
50 phrase "adjoining conforming parcel" shall mean a new site for relocation that conforms to State
51 standards and is located next to the prior site, including a parcel separated from the prior site only

10 "§ 105-153.7. Individual income tax imposed.
11 (a) Tax. – A tax is imposed for each taxable year on the North Carolina taxable income
12 of every individual. The tax shall be levied, collected, and paid annually. Except as otherwise
13 provided in subsection (a1) of this section, the The tax is a percentage of the taxpayer's North
14 Carolina taxable income computed as follows:

17 In 2023 4.75%

19 In 2025 4.25%

22 this section, if total General Fund revenue in a fiscal year set out below exceeds the trigger
23 amount indicated for that fiscal year, then the applicable tax rate for the indicated and subsequent
24 tax years shall be equal to the greater of (i) the prior taxable year's rate decreased by one-half
25 percentage point (0.50%) or (ii) two and forty-nine hundredths percent (2.49%). For purposes of
26 this subsection, total General Fund revenue is the amount stated in the final accounting of total
27 General Fund Reverting Net Tax and Non-Tax Revenues for the fiscal year, as reported by the

26 EXCLUDE CREDIT FOR TRADE-INS FROM SALES TAX
27 SECTION 44.2.(a) G.S. 105-164.3(237) reads as rewritten:
28 "(237) Sales price. – The total amount or consideration for which an item is sold,
29 leased, or rented. The consideration may be in the form of cash, credit,
30 property, or services. The sales price must be valued in money, regardless of
31 whether it is received in money.
32 a. The term includes all of the following:
33 1. The retailer's cost of the item sold.
34 2. The cost of materials used, labor or service costs, interest,
35 losses, all costs of transportation to the retailer, all taxes
36 imposed on the retailer, and any other expense of the retailer.
37 3. Charges by the retailer for any services necessary to complete
38 the sale.
39 4. Delivery charges.
40 5. Installation charges.
41 6. Repealed by Session Laws 2007-244, s. 1, effective October 1,
42 2007.
43 7. Credit for trade-in. The amount of any credit for trade-in is not
44 a reduction of the sales price.
45 8. The amount of any discounts that are reimbursable by a third
46 party and can be determined at the time of sale through any of
47 the following:
48 I. Presentation by the consumer of a coupon or other
49 documentation.
50 II. Identification of the consumer as a member of a group
51 eligible for a discount.
Senate Bill 257-Second Edition Page 435

General Assembly Of North Carolina Session 2025
1 III. The invoice the retailer gives the consumer.
2 b. The term does not include any of the following:
3 1. Discounts that are not reimbursable by a third party, are
4 allowed by the retailer, and are taken by a consumer on a sale.
5 2. Interest, financing, and carrying charges from credit extended
6 on the sale, if the amount is separately stated on the invoice,
7 bill of sale, or a similar document given to the consumer.
8 3. Any taxes imposed directly on the consumer that are separately
9 stated on the invoice, bill of sale, or similar document given to
10 the consumer.
11 4. Credit for any trade-in that is separately stated on the invoice,
12 bill of sale, or similar document given to the consumer."
13 SECTION 44.2.(b) This section is effective January 1, 2026, and applies to sales
14 occurring on or after that date.

16 INCREASE TAX BASE AMOUNT TO WHICH MAXIMUM FRANCHISE TAX
17 APPLIES FOR BOTH S AND C CORPORATIONS
18 SECTION 44.3.(a) G.S. 105-122(d2) reads as rewritten:
19 "(d2) Tax Rate. – For a C Corporation, as defined in G.S. 105-130.2, the tax rate is one
20 dollar and fifty cents ($1.50) per one thousand dollars ($1,000) of its tax base with a minimum
21 of two hundred dollars ($200.00) and a maximum of five hundred dollars ($500.00) for the first
22 one five million dollars ($1,000,000) ($5,000,000) of the corporation's its tax base as determined
23 under subsection (d) of this section. base. For an S Corporation, as defined in G.S. 105-130.2,
24 the tax rate is two hundred dollars ($200.00) for the first one five million dollars ($1,000,000)
25 ($5,000,000) of the corporation's its tax base as determined under subsection (d) of this section
26 and one dollar and fifty cents ($1.50) per one thousand dollars ($1,000) of its tax base that
27 exceeds one five million dollars ($1,000,000). In no event may the tax imposed by this section
28 be less than two hundred dollars ($200.00).($5,000,000). For purposes of this subsection, a
29 corporation's tax base is determined under subsection (d) of this section."
30 SECTION 44.3.(b) G.S. 105-120.2(b) reads as rewritten:
31 "(b) Tax Rate. – Every corporation taxed under this section shall annually pay to the
32 Secretary of Revenue, at the time the return is due, a franchise or privilege tax at the rate of one
33 dollar and fifty cents ($1.50) per one thousand dollars ($1,000) of its tax base with a maximum
34 of five hundred dollars ($500.00) for the first one million dollars ($1,000,000) of the corporation's
35 tax base as determined under subsection (a) of this section, set forth in G.S. 105-122(d2), but in
36 no case shall the tax be more than one hundred fifty thousand dollars ($150,000) nor less than
37 two hundred dollars ($200.00)."
38 SECTION 44.3.(c) This section is effective for taxable years beginning on or after
39 January 1, 2026, and is applicable to the calculation of franchise tax reported on the 2025 and
40 later corporate income tax returns.

4 Department of Revenue and reimbursement to the Lottery Commission for administrative
5 expenses, in accordance with this section. The Secretary may retain the cost of administering this
6 Article, not to exceed five hundred thousand dollars ($500,000) a year, as reimbursement to the

26 Internal Revenue Code.

28 primary purpose for the funding is to facilitate opportunities for
29 persons up to age 18 to engage in youth sports.

8 PART XLV. MISCELLANEOUS
9
10 STATE BUDGET ACT APPLIES
11 SECTION 45.1. The provisions of the State Budget Act, Chapter 143C of the
12 General Statutes, are reenacted and shall remain in full force and effect and are incorporated in
13 this act by reference.
14
15 COMMITTEE REPORT
16 SECTION 45.2.(a) The Senate Committee on Appropriations/Base Budget Report
17 on the Current Operations Appropriations Act of 2025 for Senate Bill 257 Proposed Committee
18 Substitute, as amended, which was distributed in the Senate and used to explain this act, shall
19 indicate action by the General Assembly on this act and shall, therefore, be used to construe this
20 act, as provided in the State Budget Act, Chapter 143C of the General Statutes, as appropriate,
21 and for these purposes shall be considered a part of this act and, as such, shall be printed as a part
22 of the Session Laws.
23 SECTION 45.2.(b) The budget enacted by the General Assembly is for the
24 maintenance of the various departments, institutions, and other spending agencies of the State
25 for the 2025-2027 biennial budget as provided in G.S. 143C-3-5. This budget includes the
26 appropriations of State funds as defined in G.S. 143C-1-1(d)(25).
27 The Director of the Budget submitted a recommended base budget to the General
28 Assembly in the Governor's Recommended Budget for the 2025-2027 fiscal biennium, dated
29 March 2025, and in the Budget Support Document for the various departments, institutions, and
30 other spending agencies of the State. The adjustments to the recommended base budget made by
31 the General Assembly are set out in the Committee Report.
32
33 REPORT BY FISCAL RESEARCH DIVISION
34 SECTION 45.3. The Fiscal Research Division shall issue a report on budget actions
35 taken by the 2025 Regular Session of the General Assembly. The report shall be in the form of a
36 revision of the Committee Report described in Section 45.2 of this act pursuant to G.S. 143C-5-5.
37 The Director of the Fiscal Research Division shall send a copy of the report issued pursuant to
38 this section to the Director of the Budget. The report shall be published on the General
39 Assembly's internet website for public access.
40
41 MOST TEXT APPLIES ONLY TO THE 2025-2027 FISCAL BIENNIUM
42 SECTION 45.4. Except for statutory changes or other provisions that clearly indicate
43 an intention to have effects beyond the 2025-2027 fiscal biennium, the textual provisions of this
44 act apply only to funds appropriated for, and activities occurring during, the 2025-2027 fiscal
45 biennium.
46
47 APPROPRIATIONS LIMITATIONS AND DIRECTIONS APPLY
48 SECTION 45.5. Except where expressly repealed or amended by this act, the
49 provisions of any legislation enacted during the 2025 Regular Session of the General Assembly
50 affecting the State budget shall remain in effect.
51

5
6 SEVERABILITY CLAUSE
7 SECTION 45.7. If any section or provision of this act is declared unconstitutional
8 or invalid by the courts, it does not affect the validity of this act as a whole or any part other than
9 the part so declared to be unconstitutional or invalid.
